messages.subject: RPM binaries of R 1.4.1 for RedHat/Mandrake/Linuxppc
messages.subject_clean: RPM binaries of R 1.4.1 for RedHat/Mandrake/Linuxppc
messages.body_plain:
The following RPM based binary distributions of R 1.4.1 are avaiable
on ftp://ftp-fis.iarc.fr/pub/R, and will soon be on a CRAN near mirror
near you.

Distribution Version Platform Packager
Red Hat       7.x    intel    Martyn Plummer <plummer at iarc.fr>
Red Hat       7.x    alpha    Naoki Takebayashi <ntakebay at bio.indiana.edu>
Mandrake      8.1    intel    Michele Alzetta <mikalzet at libero.it>
LinuxPPC             ppc      Alex Buerkle <buerkla at uwec.edu>

The RPMS are
R-base:        The base distribution
R-recommended: Recommended packages

Martyn Plummer

messages.subject: New package pastecs
messages.body_plain:
Hi all,

There is a new package in CRAN:

PASTECS (package for analysis of space-time ecological series).

Although primarily intended for ecologists, it should be useful to analyze
any kind of time-series. It provides several methods that are complementary
to other existing time-series libraries in CRAN. It's strength is in
regulation and decomposition of series. Some methods are quite original,
like the turnogram, Auto/CrossD2 and D2 to the center, the area method, the
distogram,...

It runs on R (Unix/Linux, Windows and Macintosh) and also on Splus.

Beside it's availability on CRAN, there are also various versions
(Splus,...) available from the web site http://www.sciviews.org/pastecs...
and shortly a 250+ pages user manual in French but that is planned to be
translated in English shortly.

Best,

Philippe Grosjean
